## Robust Soft Rooster Game Implementation

The game "Robust Soft Rooster" is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 grid, where players take turns placing roosters. The goal is to create a line of three roosters in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Definition

```ludax
(game "Robust Soft Rooster"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Rooster Movement

In this version, roosters can move one space in any direction.

```ludax
(game "Robust Soft Rooster"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (or 
                            (not (adjacent occupied))
                            (exists (adjacent empty))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Rooster Capture

In this version, a rooster can capture an opponent's rooster by jumping over it to an empty space.

```ludax
(game "Robust Soft Rooster"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (or 
                            (not (adjacent occupied))
                            (exists (adjacent empty))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Scoring

In this version, players earn points for capturing opponent roosters.

```ludax
(game "Robust Soft Rooster"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (or 
                            (not (adjacent occupied))
                            (exists (adjacent empty))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Final Version

The final version of the game includes all the features mentioned above.

```ludax
(game "Robust Soft Rooster"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (or 
                            (not (adjacent occupied))
                            (exists (adjacent empty))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

## Enigmatic Arcane Walrus Game Implementation

### Game Description

Enigmatic Arcane Walrus is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 7x7. Players take turns placing their walrus pieces on empty squares. The goal is to create a line of five walrus pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Implementation

```lark
(game "Enigmatic Arcane Walrus"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Scoring System

In this version, we add a scoring system. Players earn points for each line of three or more walrus pieces they create.

```lark
(game "Enigmatic Arcane Walrus"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover (line 3 exact:true))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Capture Mechanic

In this version, we add a capture mechanic. Players can capture their opponent's walrus pieces by surrounding them.

```lark
(game "Enigmatic Arcane Walrus"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Forced Pass Mechanic

In this version, we add a forced pass mechanic. Players are forced to pass their turn if they have no available moves.

```lark
(game "Enigmatic Arcane Walrus"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Independent Refined Hedgehog Game Implementation

The Independent Refined Hedgehog game is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 11x11. The objective of the game is to create a connected line of 5 or more hedgehogs in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Rules

1. The game starts with an empty board.
2. Player 1 (P1) places the first hedgehog on the board.
3. Players alternate turns.
4. On each turn, a player can place a hedgehog on an empty square.
5. A player cannot place a hedgehog on a square that is already occupied.
6. The game ends when a player creates a connected line of 5 or more hedgehogs in any direction.
7. If the board is completely filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Independent Refined Hedgehog"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

* The game is defined with two players and an 11x11 square board.
* The `play` section defines the game logic, where players alternate turns placing hedgehogs on empty squares.
* The `end` section defines the game-ending conditions, where a player wins if they create a connected line of 5 or more hedgehogs, and the game is a draw if the board is completely filled.

### Incremental Additions

To make the game more interesting, we can add additional rules:

* **Capturing opponent's hedgehogs**: A player can capture an opponent's hedgehog by placing their own hedgehog adjacent to it.
* **Hedgehog movement**: Hedgehogs can move one square in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) on a player's turn.

These additions can be implemented using Ludax's built-in functions and predicates.

### Capturing Opponent's Hedgehogs

```ludax
(game "Independent Refined Hedgehog"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Hedgehog Movement

```ludax
(game "Independent Refined Hedgehog"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (move (adjacent occupied mover) (destination empty))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

These additions enhance the gameplay and strategy of the Independent Refined Hedgehog game.

# Imperial Military Mantis Game Implementation in Ludax

## Game Description

Imperial Military Mantis is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a set of rules that allow players to move and capture pieces.

## Simplified Version

### Game Definition

```ludax
(game "Imperial Military Mantis"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Incremental Addition of Rules

### Adding Start Rules

```ludax
(game "Imperial Military Mantis"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Play Effects

```ludax
(game "Imperial Military Mantis"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Win Conditions

```ludax
(game "Imperial Military Mantis"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (= (count (occupied P1)) 10) (P1 win))
            (if (= (count (occupied P2)) 10) (P2 win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Complete Game Implementation

```ludax
(game "Imperial Military Mantis"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (= (count (occupied P1)) 10) (P1 win))
            (if (= (count (occupied P2)) 10) (P2 win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

# Remarkable Ladybug Of Fantasy

## Game Description

Remarkable Ladybug Of Fantasy is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 7x7. The objective of the game is to create a line of 5 ladybugs in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

## Game Implementation in Ludax

```lark
(game "Remarkable Ladybug Of Fantasy"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    )

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

In this version, we add a start rule to place the first ladybug in the center of the board.


## Adding Play Effects

In this version, we add a play effect to capture an opponent's ladybug when a player places a ladybug adjacent to an opponent's ladybug.

## Adding End Rules

```lark
(game "Remarkable Ladybug Of Fantasy"
    (players 2)

        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)

                )
```

## Meaty Urban Numbat Game Implementation

### Game Description

Meaty Urban Numbat is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a set of rules that involve placing and capturing pieces.

### Simplified Rules

The simplified rules for Meaty Urban Numbat are as follows:

* The game is played on a 5x5 rectangular board.
* Player 1 (P1) starts by placing a piece on the center square of the board.
* Players alternate turns, with each player placing one piece per turn on an empty square.
* A player can capture an opponent's piece by placing one of their pieces on an adjacent square.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Meaty Urban Numbat"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Rule Additions

#### Rule 1: Piece Movement

* A player can move one of their pieces to an adjacent square.

```ludax
(game "Meaty Urban Numbat"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (place
                    (destination (adjacent occupied mover))
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

#### Rule 2: Piece Promotion

* When a player places a piece on a square occupied by an opponent's piece, the opponent's piece is removed from the board and the player's score is incremented.

```ludax
(game "Meaty Urban Numbat"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (place
                    (destination (adjacent occupied mover))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
)
```

### Complete Game Implementation

The complete game implementation with all the rules is as follows:

```ludax
(game "Meaty Urban Numbat"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (place
                    (destination (adjacent occupied mover))
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

## Solemn Ingenious Starling

### Game Description

Solemn Ingenious Starling is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing stones on empty squares. The game ends when one player has five stones in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. If all squares are filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Solemn Ingenious Starling"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or (line 5 orientation:horizontal) (line 5 orientation:vertical) (line 5 orientation:diagonal)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Rule Additions

#### 1. Capturing Pieces

In this version, a player can capture an opponent's piece by placing their own piece adjacent to it.

```lark
(game "Solemn Ingenious Starling"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or (line 5 orientation:horizontal) (line 5 orientation:vertical) (line 5 orientation:diagonal)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

#### 2. Scoring

In this version, players earn points for capturing opponent pieces.

```lark
(game "Solemn Ingenious Starling"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or (line 5 orientation:horizontal) (line 5 orientation:vertical) (line 5 orientation:diagonal)) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

#### 3. Forced Pass

In this version, a player must pass if they have no valid moves.

```lark
(game "Solemn Ingenious Starling"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or (line 5 orientation:horizontal) (line 5 orientation:vertical) (line 5 orientation:diagonal)) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Chubby Doberman Of Opposition

### Game Description

Chubby Doberman Of Opposition is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 4x4 square board. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board, trying to create a line of three pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Simplified Rules

The simplified rules of the game are as follows:

* The game is played on a 4x4 square board.
* Players take turns placing their pieces on the board.
* A player wins if they create a line of three pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Ludax Implementation

Here is a simplified implementation of the game in Ludax:
```lark
(game "Chubby Doberman Of Opposition"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 4))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```
This implementation defines a 4x4 square board and allows players to take turns placing their pieces on the board. A player wins if they create a line of three pieces in a row.

### Adding More Rules

To make the game more interesting, we can add more rules. For example, we can add a rule that a player can only place their piece on a square that is adjacent to an opponent's piece.

Here is an updated implementation of the game:
```lark
(game "Chubby Doberman Of Opposition"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 4))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent occupied)))
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```
This implementation adds a rule that a player can only place their piece on a square that is adjacent to an opponent's piece.

### Adding More Complex Rules

To make the game even more complex, we can add more rules. For example, we can add a rule that a player can capture an opponent's piece by placing their own piece on a square that is diagonally adjacent to the opponent's piece.

Here is an updated implementation of the game:
```lark
(game "Chubby Doberman Of Opposition"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 4))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent occupied)))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (diagonal occupied)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```
This implementation adds a rule that a player can capture an opponent's piece by placing their own piece on a square that is diagonally adjacent to the opponent's piece.

Note that the Ludax language is very expressive and allows for complex rules to be defined. However, the complexity of the rules can make the game more difficult to understand and play.

## Resilient Mouse Of Democracy

### Game Description

Resilient Mouse Of Democracy is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 5x5. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. The goal is to create a connected line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Simplified Rules

We will start by implementing a simplified version of the game.

```ludax
(game "Resilient Mouse Of Democracy"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

Let's add a start rule to place the first piece of player 1 in the center of the board.

```ludax
(game "Resilient Mouse Of Democracy"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Play Effects

Let's add a play effect to capture adjacent pieces of the opponent.

```ludax
(game "Resilient Mouse Of Democracy"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding End Rules

Let's add an end rule to check if the mover has won by creating a connected line of five pieces.

```ludax
(game "Resilient Mouse Of Democracy"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (and (line 5) (mover_is P1)) (P1 win))
            (if (and (line 5) (mover_is P2)) (P2 win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

This is a basic implementation of the Resilient Mouse Of Democracy game in Ludax. You can add more rules and features as per your requirements.

## Visionary Laughing Tarantula Game Implementation

The Visionary Laughing Tarantula game is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 7x7. The objective of the game is to create a line of 5 or more pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Rules

* The game starts with an empty board.
* Player 1 (P1) places their piece on a random empty square.
* Player 2 (P2) places their piece on a random empty square.
* Players alternate turns.
* On each turn, a player can place their piece on an empty square.
* A player can only place their piece on a square that is adjacent to an opponent's piece.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Visionary Laughing Tarantula"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Additional Rules

To make the game more interesting, we can add additional rules:

* A player can capture an opponent's piece by placing their piece on an adjacent square.
* A player can only capture an opponent's piece if they have at least two pieces adjacent to the opponent's piece.

```lark
(game "Visionary Laughing Tarantula"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Rendering Details

To make the game more visually appealing, we can add rendering details:

```lark
(game "Visionary Laughing Tarantula"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

prompt:
Invent simple rules for a novel two player abstract strategy game called Poised Leech Of Elevation. Implement it in the ludax language. You will find attached the ludax's grammar as well as a few examples of games implemented in ludax. Start by implementing a simplified version of your rules, and then incrementally add rules that are harder to express in ludax. At each step, make sure you write a compilable game according to ludax's grammar.

        grammar.lark:
// ---Root---
game: "(" "game" name players equipment rules rendering? ")"

// ---Players---
players: "(" "players" positive_int ")"

// ---Equipment---
equipment: "(" "equipment" board ")"
board: "(" "board" (board_square | board_rectangle | board_hexagon | board_hex_rectangle) ")"
board_square: "(" "square" number ")"
board_rectangle: "(" "rectangle" number number ")"
board_hexagon: "(" "hexagon"  number ")"
board_hex_rectangle: "(" "hex_rectangle" number number ")"

// ---Rules---
rules: "(" "rules" start_rules? play_rules end_rules ")"

// ---Start rules---
start_rules: "(" "start" start_rule+ ")"
start_rule: start_place
start_place: "(" "place" player_reference pattern_arg ")"

// ---Play rules---
play_rules: "(" "play" play_phase+ ")"
play_phase: phase_once_through | phase_repeat
phase_once_through: "(" "once_through" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
phase_repeat: "(" "repeat" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
play_mover_order: "(" player_reference+ ")"

play_mechanic: play_place force_pass? 
play_place: "(" "place" mover_reference? place_destination_constraint place_result_constraint? play_effects?")"
force_pass: "(" "force_pass" ")"

// ---Constraints---
place_destination_constraint: "(" "destination" super_mask ")"
place_result_constraint: "(" "result" super_predicate ")"

// ---Effects---
play_effects: "(" "effects" play_effect+ ")"
play_effect: effect_capture 
           | effect_flip
           | effect_increment_score
           | effect_set_score

effect_capture: "(" "capture" super_mask mover_reference? increment_score_arg? ")"
effect_flip: "(" "flip" super_mask mover_reference? ")"
effect_increment_score: "(" "increment_score" mover_reference function ")"
effect_set_score: "(" "set_score" mover_reference function ")"

// ---Functions---
function: function_add
        | function_connected
        | function_constant
        | function_count
        | function_line
        | function_multiply
        | function_score
        | function_subtract

function_add: "(" "add" function+ ")"
function_connected: "(" "connected" multi_mask_arg mover_reference? direction_arg? ")"
function_constant: positive_int
function_count: "(" "count" super_mask ")"
function_line: "(" "line" positive_int orientation_arg? exact_arg? ")"
function_multiply: "(" "multiply" function+ ")"
function_score: "(" "score" mover_reference ")"
function_subtract: "(" "subtract" function function ")"

// ---End rules---
end_rules: "(" "end" end_rule+ ")"
end_rule: "(" "if" super_predicate end_rule_result ")"
?end_rule_result: result_win | result_lose | result_draw | result_by_score

// -- Result definitions --
result_win: "(" mover_reference "win" ")"
result_lose: "(" mover_reference "lose" ")"
result_draw: "(" "draw" ")"
result_by_score: "(" "by_score" ")"

// -- Mask definitions --
super_mask: mask | super_mask_and | super_mask_or | super_mask_not
super_mask_and: "(" "and" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_or: "(" "or" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_not: "(" "not" super_mask ")"

mask: mask_adjacent
    | mask_center
    | mask_corners
    | mask_custodial
    | mask_edge
    | mask_empty
    | mask_occupied
    | mask_pattern
    | mask_prev_move

mask_adjacent: "(" "adjacent" super_mask direction_arg? ")"
mask_center: "center"
mask_corners: "corners"
mask_custodial: "(" "custodial" custodial_length_arg mover_reference? orientation_arg? ")"
mask_edge: "(" "edge" edge ")"
mask_empty: "empty"
mask_occupied: "occupied" | "(" "occupied" mover_reference ")"
mask_pattern: "(" "pattern" dimensions_arg pattern_arg rotate_arg? ")"
mask_prev_move: "(" "prev_move" mover_reference ")"

// "Multi-masks" are special keywords that are manually split into multiple
// sub-masks at compile time. This is mostly useful for the "connected" function,
// which expects a list of masks to check for connections between
multi_mask: multi_mask_corners
          | multi_mask_edges
          | multi_mask_edges_no_corners

multi_mask_corners: "corners"
multi_mask_edges: "edges"
multi_mask_edges_no_corners: "edgesNoCorners"

// ---Predicate definitions---
super_predicate: predicate | super_predicate_and | super_predicate_or | super_predicate_not
super_predicate_and: "(" "and" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_or: "(" "or" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_not: "(" "not" super_predicate ")"

predicate: predicate_equals
         | predicate_exists
         | predicate_full_board
         | predicate_function
         | predicate_greater_equals
         | predicate_less_equals
         | predicate_mover_is
         | predicate_passed

predicate_equals: "(" "=" function+ ")"
predicate_exists: "(" "exists" super_mask ")" // technically equivalent to (>= (count mask) 1) 
predicate_full_board: "(" "full_board" ")"
predicate_function: function // special syntax which is equivalent to "(>= function 1)"
predicate_greater_equals: "(" ">=" function function ")"
predicate_less_equals: "(" "<=" function function ")"
predicate_mover_is: "(" "mover_is" player_reference ")"
predicate_passed: "(" "passed" (mover_reference | BOTH) ")"

// Additional (potentially optional) arguments for predicates
custodial_length_arg: ANY | positive_int
dimensions_arg: "(" positive_int positive_int ")"
direction_arg: "direction:" direction
exact_arg: "exact:" boolean
increment_score_arg: "increment_score:" boolean
multi_mask_arg: multi_mask | "(" super_mask+ ")"
orientation_arg: "orientation:" orientation
pattern_arg: "(" positive_int+ ")"
rotate_arg: "rotate:" boolean

// Optional rendering details
rendering: "(" "rendering" rendering_detail+ ")"
rendering_detail: color_assignment

color_assignment: "(" "color" player_reference color ")"

// General-purpose definitions
?number: SIGNED_NUMBER
?positive_int: /[0-9]+/
?boolean: TRUE | FALSE
?edge: TOP | BOTTOM | LEFT | RIGHT | TOP_LEFT | TOP_RIGHT | BOTTOM_LEFT | BOTTOM_RIGHT
?direction: UP | DOWN | LEFT | RIGHT | UP_LEFT | UP_RIGHT | DOWN_LEFT | DOWN_RIGHT | VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?orientation: VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?color: WHITE | BLACK
// ----------------------------

?player_reference: P1 | P2
?mover_reference: MOVER | OPPONENT
name: STRING
variable_name: /\?[a-z][a-z0-9]*/
id: /[a-zA-Z0-9_]+/

// Constants
TOP: "top"
BOTTOM: "bottom"
UP: "up"
DOWN: "down"
LEFT: "left"
RIGHT: "right"
TOP_LEFT: "top_left"
TOP_RIGHT: "top_right"
BOTTOM_LEFT: "bottom_left"
BOTTOM_RIGHT: "bottom_right"
UP_LEFT: "up_left"
UP_RIGHT: "up_right"
DOWN_LEFT: "down_left"
DOWN_RIGHT: "down_right"
VERTICAL: "vertical"
HORIZONTAL: "horizontal"
ORTHOGONAL: "orthogonal"
DIAGONAL: "diagonal"
BACK_DIAGONAL: "back_diagonal"
FORWARD_DIAGONAL: "forward_diagonal"
ANY: "any"
TRUE: "true"
FALSE: "false"
MOVER: "mover"
OPPONENT: "opponent"
P1: "P1"
P2: "P2"
BOTH: "both"
WHITE: "white"
BLACK: "black"

// Lark imports
%import common.ESCAPED_STRING -> STRING
%import common.SIGNED_NUMBER
%import common.WS
%ignore WS


        example games:
        

(game "Complexity Demo" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Four"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 6 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination (and empty (or (edge bottom) (adjacent occupied direction:up)))))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Six"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 6) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Hex"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hex_rectangle 11 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Gomoku" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 15))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5 exact:true) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Pente" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination center))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Reversi" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (28 35))
            (place P2 (27 36))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (exists
                            (custodial any)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)


(game "Tic-Tac-Toe" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 3))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalath" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hexagon 9))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play 
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover lose))
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalax" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 13))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (and 
                            (not (line 5))
                            (not (= (line 4) 1))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 4) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
